title: RRDI rice variety ladder — picking by maturity, not just yield
body: Sri Lanka's DOA/RRDI recommended rice portfolio is organised by maturity period, not by absolute yield — because the right variety depends on how many months of water a farmer reliably has. The 2.5-month group (Bg 750, Bg 250, Bg 251 GSR, Bg 252, Ld 253, At 313) is the short-duration ladder for tail-end irrigation blocks and late-Yala conditions. The 3-month group (H 10, Bg 300s + At 300s families, Bw 302) is the default for most irrigated tanks. The 3.5-month group is the largest and includes the popular Bg 352, At 353, Bg 357–360 (Nadu grain family), and Bg 366/369/370 — good for reservoir-fed mainland production. The 4–4.5-month group (Bg 379-2, Bg 400-1, At 405, Bg 450, Bg 454/455) is for lower-elevation long-season fields. Two-letter prefixes encode the breeding station: Bg = Batalagoda, At = Ambalantota, Bw = Bombuwela, Ld = Labuduwa, H = hybrid, MI = Maha Illuppallama. Choose by irrigation timeline first, grain market second.
